# terrible_cisco_dnac_wireless_settings_ap_profiles_id (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `apPowerProfileName` (String) Name of the existing AP power profile.
- `apProfileName` (String) Name of the Access Point profile. Max length is 32 characters.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `awipsEnabled` (Boolean) Indicates if AWIPS is enabled on the AP.
- `awipsForensicEnabled` (Boolean) Indicates if AWIPS forensic is enabled on the AP. Forensic Capture is supported from IOS-XE version 17.4 and above. Forensic Capture can be activated only if aWIPS is enabled.
- `calendarPowerProfiles` (String) Wireless Settings Ap Profiles Id's calendarPowerProfiles.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `clientLimit` (Number) Number of clients. Value should be between 0-1200.
- `countryCode` (String) Country Code.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description of the AP profile. Max length is 241 characters.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `managementSetting` (String) Wireless Settings Ap Profiles Id's managementSetting.
- `meshEnabled` (Boolean) This indicates whether mesh networking is enabled on the AP. For IOS-XE devices, when mesh networking is enabled, a custom mesh profile with the configured parameters will be created and mapped to the AP join profile on the device. When mesh networking is disabled, any existing custom mesh profile will be deleted from the device, and the AP join profile will be mapped to the default mesh profile on the device.
- `meshSetting` (String) Wireless Settings Ap Profiles Id's meshSetting.
- `pmfDenialEnabled` (Boolean) Indicates if PMF denial is active on the AP. PMF Denial is supported from IOS-XE version 17.12 and above.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `remoteWorkerEnabled` (Boolean) Indicates if remote worker mode is enabled on the AP. Remote teleworker enabled profile cannot support security features like aWIPS,Forensic Capture Enablement, Rogue Detection and Rogue Containment.
- `rogueDetectionSetting` (String) Wireless Settings Ap Profiles Id's rogueDetectionSetting.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeZone` (String) In the Time Zone area, choose one of the following options. Not Configured - APs operate in the UTC time zone. Controller - APs operate in the Cisco Wireless Controller time zone. Delta from Controller - APs operate in the offset time from the wireless controller time zone.
- `timeZoneOffsetHour` (Number) Enter the hour value (HH). The valid range is from -12 through 14.
- `timeZoneOffsetMinutes` (Number) Enter the minute value (MM). The valid range is from 0 through 59.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `dnac_response` (String) A dictionary or list with the response returned by the Cisco DNAC Python SDK
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
